video_title: Top #DDD Videos in San Francisco with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Pier 23
restaurants.guy_intro: Not in San Francisco on the Embarcadero. Now, this bad boy's all mine because my crew and I have been hanging here for years. I mean, we're talking fresh seafood, live music, a huge patio, and you know what? Good times. You got to check out Pier 23.

dishes.description: Dungeness crab boiled and ice-bathed, then cooked in a hot pan with olive oil, salt, pepper, garlic, parsley, white wine, and butter. Served with sourdough bread.
dishes.guy_response: That is just awesome. This has got the lemon juice, but it's also got some of that great crab flavor. And the white wine. This is getting messy. I mean, this isn't for the timid. ... It's really, really good. It's always really fresh. Lemon, butter, garlic sauce. I love it.

restaurants.name: Nopalito
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in San Francisco, California, in an area that I have driven through a million times on the way to the airport, but never visited. It's called the Inner Sunset District. Now, right across the street, Golden Gate Park. Five miles that way, Ocean Beach. And I'm here to check out a Mexican joint that's doing it real deal. This is Nopalito.

dishes.description: Carnitas (pork cooked with oranges, onions, garlic, cinnamon, bay leaves, lard, Mexican sugar, milk, dark beer) and chicharrones (pork skin poached, dehydrated, fried) stuffed into a red, flavored tortilla with jack cheese, then oven-baked. Topped with queso fresco, cilantro, and onions.
dishes.guy_response: The flavor's outrageous. Light, crispy tortilla. Yeah. That's the queso fresco quicharron. A pork that is super rich. It's not just regular carnitas. Unusually, quesadilla is most often boring. This is everything but boring. Wow. Right. That level, phenomenal. Reminds me of my grandmother's carnita.

restaurants.name: Grand Harbor
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in South San Francisco, about 10 minutes from SFO Airport. We're about to check out one of the biggest Triple D joints ever, seating over 450 people at a time. Oh, yes, the dim sum's fantastic. But we're here because it's owned by one of our favorite Triple D chefs, his brother. This is Grand Harbor.

dishes.description: Chicken feet cleaned, claws cut off, boiled, fried, then boiled again for an hour. Mixed with a sauce made from soy sauce, chicken base, salt, sugar, cornstarch, food coloring, jalapenos, and Fresnos, then steamed.
dishes.guy_response: Dang. The flavor is killer. It's super tender. So good. You get all the skin, the fat, the little bit of meat. It's creamy. Just forget that it's a chicken foot. Dude, I'm impressed. The texture is not what you would expect. A little sauce. Really tasty.

restaurants.name: Pica Pica
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Northern California in one of my favorite cities, San Francisco. Now, it's been a while since we've had the Venezuelan specialty arepas on the show. You know, we're talking about those grilled corn pockets that are stuffed with goodness. So you know I am stoked to find out right here in the Mission District, there's a joint that's rocking the arepas. This is Pica Pica.

dishes.description: Grilled corn pocket stuffed with carne mechada (shredded chuck beef boiled with cilantro, onions, red peppers, garlic, salt), black bean paste, tajadas (sweet plantains), and Venezuelan queso fresco. The beef is cooked in a pabellón sauce (sofrito, sugarcane, cumin, Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, mustard, salt, ketchup, tomatoes).
dishes.guy_response: You bite into this, and you get the exterior of the crunch, and then it's creamy, and really creamy. Wow. I've had a lot of arepas. That's the biggest one by far, but that meat, it's outrageous. The depth of flavor in this, you get sweet, you get tang, you get sour, and then you clean your palate with a little bit of this slaw. I don't know what to tell you, girl. That's outrageous. I mean, like, outrageous, outrageous. The right amount of sweet, a little bit of spice, and then the queso fresco is the perfect finish.

restaurants.name: The Front Porch
restaurants.guy_intro: Well, here I am in the southeast side of the Mission District to check out a joint that started in the 30s as an Italian restaurant. In a garage. Well, fast forward to 2006, and now it's a funky little joint that's serving up some killer southern comfort food. Oh, that's right, you gotta come kick it at the front porch.

dishes.description: Stone ground grits cooked with whole milk, water, unsalted butter, black pepper, kosher salt, and sharp cheddar cheese. Served with wild gulf shrimp poached in a red-eye gravy (canola oil, shallots, garlic, andouille sausage, Creole spice, Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco, chicory coffee, chicken stock). Garnished with green onions.
dishes.guy_response: This isn't exactly how I know red-eye gravy to be where I'm from but I will tell you it's awesome got a little coffee kick I mean it's rich grits are cheesy the andouille is spot-on everything going on in this is outstanding but really the crescendo is a fresh shrimp I mean succulent have I ever used that word on Triple D oh my gosh killer dish cool I'll see you next time.

restaurants.name: Señor Sisig
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in San Francisco in the Soma district south of Market to look for a food truck now I hear these guys are doing a cross between Filipino and Latin American cuisine you never know where they're gonna be so you got to check them out on their website and I found them this is senior seasick

dishes.description: Chicken drumettes and wings marinated in soy sauce, sugarcane vinegar, garlic, bay leaves, and crushed peppercorn for five hours. Fire-baked halfway, then tossed in an adobo glaze (olive oil, garlic, peppercorn, bay leaves, soy sauce, sugarcane vinegar, chipotle, ketchup, brown sugar, cayenne pepper, red chili flakes, Worcestershire sauce, salt, cornstarch slurry, water). Served on a bed of shrimp chips, garnished with sesame seeds and green onion, lemon zest.
dishes.guy_response: I can't figure out the flavor. All of a sudden I got soy sauce. Now I get chipotle. Now I get a little bit of the garlic. Now I get sweet. Yeah. The wings are cooked perfectly. Little big wings. Great crust to them. And the shrimp chips as accompaniment. Right on, for sure. Not too sweet, not too spicy. Not Asian, not Mexican. Just senior seasick.
